text.title: typescript-react-2026 Failure Modes
text.summary: Four 2026-stack antipatterns the spec must forbid.

antipattern.symptom: Whole dashboard route ships ~80 KB of JS, no streaming, no static render. Lighthouse RSC bytes counter near zero.
antipattern.root-cause: app/dashboard/layout.tsx starts with `'use client'` (a developer added it to "make the sidebar's useState work"). Every descendant is now a client component.
antipattern.fix: Remove the directive from the layout. Move the stateful sidebar into its own file (`app/dashboard/Sidebar.tsx`) with `'use client'` at the top. The layout composes <Sidebar /> as a child. Spec field routes[].files[].boundary MUST not be "use-client" for files matching *layout.tsx.

antipattern.symptom: Migration to React 19 produces deprecation warnings; on the next major version, the form crashes at boot ("useFormState is not a function").
antipattern.root-cause: Code imports `useFormState` from 'react-dom'. This is the legacy React 18 name; React 19 renamed the hook to `useActionState`.
antipattern.fix: Replace import + call site with `useActionState`. The signature is identical. Spec MUST NOT contain references to useFormState anywhere.

antipattern.symptom: User submits the form, gets a success toast, but the list still shows the old data. Refresh fixes it.
antipattern.root-cause: The Server Action mutated the DB and returned `{ success: true }` without calling `revalidatePath` or `revalidateTag`. Next.js's default fetch cache served the stale list on the next read.
antipattern.fix: Add `revalidatePath('/invoices')` (or revalidateTag('invoices')) before returning. Spec actions[].revalidate.kind MUST equal "path" or "tag" when mutates=true.

antipattern.symptom: Runtime TypeError "Cannot read property of undefined" on a route that worked yesterday; the data shape didn't change but the array was empty.
antipattern.root-cause: Code uses `users[0]!.email` while noUncheckedIndexedAccess is on. The `!` told TS to trust the developer; the array was empty in this request and the assertion was wrong.
antipattern.fix: Destructure with default: `const [first] = users; if (!first) return …`. Or use optional chaining: `users[0]?.email ?? 'unknown'`. Lint rule: ban `!` on any indexed access expression.
